items.title: The Witcher 3
items.note: The first game I played where I felt like the world would keep going if I walked away. Still the peak of narrative RPGs for me.

items.title: Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
items.note: Still installing mods 13 years later. At this point the modding is the game.

items.title: Baldur's Gate 3
items.note: First CRPG that made me actually read all the dialogue. 200 hours in and still finding new things.

items.title: Pathfinder: WotR
items.note: Harder, meaner, and twice as long as BG3. Finished the Lich path. Felt evil the entire time.

items.title: League of Legends
items.note: Six years. Probably net negative for my mental health. Still queuing up.

items.title: Teamfight Tactics
items.note: The version of League I play when I want to lose slowly and feel smart about it.

items.title: Monster Hunter: World
items.note: This game's mechanics are overcomplicated for no reason and I love it, 500hrs of bowing and miles away from being decent at it, Alatreon is a bitch (a bad one), story fucking sucks, so do people, that's why I never did Kulve and Safi

items.title: Clair Obscur: E33
items.note: Watched every cutscene, read every lore thread, know the OST by heart. Never touched the controller. Still counts.

items.title: Asmongold
items.note: Loud, unfiltered, weirdly right about the gaming industry more often than he should be.

items.title: Sodapoppin
items.note: Has been entertaining me for a decade. I don't think about why, it just works.

items.title: Moonmoon
items.note: The exact energy of a guy who's too good at everything but acts like he isn't.

items.title: Mortismal Gaming
items.note: The only reviewer I trust for CRPGs. If he says it's good, it's good.

items.title: Lirik
items.note: For when I need background noise that occasionally becomes genuinely funny.

items.title: Cơm Tấm
items.note: Broken rice, grilled pork, fried egg, pickles. The math of a perfect meal.

items.title: Cà Phê Đen
items.note: Black, no sugar, no ice. If it needs anything else it wasn't coffee to begin with.

items.title: Bánh Mì
items.note: Best food-to-price ratio in existence. This is not up for debate.

items.title: Bún Riêu
items.note: The underrated one. Crab broth, tomato, tofu. Most people sleep on it.

items.title: Bún Đậu Mắm Tôm
items.note: Not for everyone. That's exactly why it's for me.

items.title: Claude Code
items.note: Runs in my terminal. Does things I'd spend hours on in seconds. Bit unsettling.

meta.reason: Thinks in context, not just completions

items.title: Obsidian
items.note: Everything I've ever thought is in here somewhere. Local files, no subscription, no excuses.

items.title: VS Code
items.note: Still the baseline. Extensions do the rest.

meta.reason: Everything else is built on top of this

items.title: Marvel Phase 1–3
items.note: Phase 1–3 was a genuine cultural event. Watching Endgame opening night was something I won't forget.

items.title: Game of Thrones
items.note: The first 6 seasons are some of the best television ever made. I've decided to stop there.

items.title: The Boys
items.note: Superheroes as a corporate PR problem. Makes me laugh and feel bad about laughing.

items.title: House of the Dragon
items.note: The dragons are cool and all, but it's the Targaryen family drama that hooks me. Also, Rhaenyra is a queen and you can't convince me otherwise.

items.title: Phàm Nhân Tu Tiên
items.note: The one that made me understand why people read cultivation novels. Part 1 is perfect — earned power, real stakes, no inflation.

items.title: Đại Đạo Triều Thiên
items.note: The more I read, the more I need the logic to hold. This one holds.
